developer_response.body:
Thank you for the feedback, and glad you’re enjoying the game!

“Balance resources” generally means not using all the collected points/resources shown on the drone just to keep building and upgrading turrets everywhere. Part of the challenge is managing those resources carefully while still collecting the Required Target points (should be displayed on the screen) needed to complete the mission/level.

Hope that helps! Feel free to join us on Discord where other players and the dev team can help further if needed:
Xlab Digital Discord : https://xlabdigital.com/discord

reviews.review_title: Not a classic tower defense game
reviews.review_description: Well made, good graphics and audio - 5 stars for this - but too difficult, without the possibility of adjusting the difficulty and remove the flying drones. The game was fun up to level 7, up to the aircrafts. For me it's way to difficult to manage the TD part and also shoot the drones. I feel nausea when having to move around to dodge the drones. Not enought resources to buy the upgrades to build the specific defense against the aircrafts ... so I quit. Sorry but it's too difficult. Level 7 for me is the wall. I don't know why in hell there are no cheats for VR ...

developer_response.body:
Hi!
Sorry to hear you had a tough time with the enemy drones, they can definitely be challenging! Here are a few tips that might help:

Keep your distance (jump to the other end of the map) and pick them off one by one to avoid getting overwhelmed.

Try using a charged shot by holding the trigger — when drones group up, a supercharged shot can take out several at once.

Use the rocket turrets (if you have bought it) — they’ll automatically target and take down enemy drones.

And don’t forget about the superweapon (artillery strike) — it’s perfect for wiping out clusters of enemy drones in one go.

Hopefully, these tips make things a little easier and help you enjoy the rest of the game!
Feel free to join our Discord — other players and our devs are always happy to share strategies and help out.

Happy gaming!

reviews.review_description: One of the best games for VR.

This is my first tower defense game.  It is great.  A nice game to just chill and play.  A few levels took me several times to "crack the code" strategy wise. But that is what makes it fun and VERY addicting.  Gonna go back and try to 3 star all the levels one I get all the upgrades. Looking forward to Salvation.
Had the trial and decided to buy the game. Really enjoyable so will be the new version when released.

I really enjoyed the game mechanics of being able to build road blocks to funnel the enemy into a kill zone. I love that you can shoot at the enemy waves with the drone ship, and the flying enemy vessels that can swarm you. I really thought the environments looked very science fiction and well done. I did get a bit frustrated with some of the harder levels, but realized sometimes relying on the basic weapons is the best strategy, and was able to get to level 30 and unlock infinite wave mode which is really fun. Great game and definitely worth the cost!

Love the game.  Mechanics are solid.  Very easy to play and figure out. Challenging amd very fun. I recommend this game.  But this is the type of game I enjoy.  As it's a sit down reminds me of command and Conquer alittle
All the feel of old school single-player RTS in VR. Definitely worth your time if you like this genre. Look up any reviews on YouTube if you're curious.

Game progresses well.
Engaging gameplay and a believable story unfolds as you progress.
Offers some rewarding replay to gain 3-star status on each level, some however will test you wit’s doing this.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind review, so glad you enjoyed the story, it's often looked over but we enjoyed weaving that into the game, and have taken it to the next level in the sequel IG:Salvation, along with many new features, mechanics and game modes, hope you enjoy it. Happy gaming !!

reviews.review_description:
I had a lot of fun with the game until I got to level 22. No matter how many times and what I tried, the 13th wave overran me. I used a lot of lasers. The problem was that they didn't shoot at the robots that were close to the base, but at those that were less dangerously close. Still a very good game, even if it ends for me personally at level 22.

PS: 
Hey developers, have you nerfed the (really difficult) 22nd level a bit? Don't think I suddenly became so super good. Without hope, I tried it again, and on the second attempt: mission completed :)
Finally, it's moving on.

developer_response.body:
Hey Christian667, thank you for the kind review! If you want to give level 22 another shot, there is a YouTube video by SBAtomic that could give you some inspiration on how you want to tackle that level again. Thank you so much for playing our game. More is coming on the horizon! 

- The IRON GUARD team

reviews.review_description:
This is the game that really captured me with my Quest 3. It is really engaging, and requires both active engagement as well as some planning and analysis.

It also seems quite flawless, I did not spot any bugs except for the drone status being hidden/corrupted on level 30. I was thinking perhaps it is intentional to make things more difficult, but it seems more of a glitch. Anyhow, it did not break the gameplay, and it was cool to see the team going home successfully.

I am eagerly awaiting the sequel, and although I got this on sale, I will not wait for a sale for the sequel. I would buy it even at $60 :-)
I just hope the sequel will utilize the Quest 3 graphics quality potential fully. I did not feel that Iron Guard was quite there compared to other more recent games like Moss.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the wonderful review and maybe the only one without a spoiler alert 😁

The level 30 "drone status being hidden/corrupted" is part of the story and explained at the start of the level with the VO and dialogue box , in short it's on purpose to tie into the story and give a unique challenge to the players (if you focus you can see the drone status update randomly in English )  

Looking forward to your feedback on IG:Salvation ☺️

reviews.review_description: Love the strategy of this tower attack genre game. Tons of replay ability  Reminds me of Warcraft 2 mixed with Command and Conqueor.

reviews.review_title: Shooter disguised as Tower Defense game
reviews.review_description:
Played the free trial, was having a lot of fun, so I bought it. Unfortunately, the trial didn’t convey how important shooting is. You can’t just build stuff and watch the wave. Personally, I’ve never played a tower defense game and thought, “I like it, but it needs more shooting”.  It’s fine as something “extra” you can do, but I’m not interested in a design where to clear a level you have to rapidly swivel and shoot enemies coming from 4 directions at once. At this point, it’s no longer a tower defense game, it’s a shooter. And if I wanted to be playing a shooter, I’d be playing a shooter. 
My other beef with the design is that the way I’ve always played TD is to identify the choke points and prioritize those spots by building and upgrading there asap. My experience so far is that the upgraded units are underpowered. Upgrades should be incentived, but I’m seeing too many situations where putting a new unit at a non-choke point is more effective than upgrading a facility at a choke point. This feels very wrong, and it’s forcing me to play in a way that feels different (in a bad way) than every other TD game I’ve ever played. 
All this said, it is super addictive and I’m still enjoying it; I’m just very frustrated at some of the design choices.

Edit: Changing my review to 1 star. I’ve grown to despise this game. I wanted a tower defense game but in the deeper levels it’s all about frenetic shooting and trial and error bs. There’s no strategy, not even a grind-to-win mechanic; just guess what gate config or build/upgrade sequence will work on a given level, and then shoot everything as fast as you can.

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks for the feedback and sorry to hear the game is not meeting your expectations. We'll share the feedback with our dev team for the future. Do join us on our discord channel to chat with devs and other players.
Each level is designed to be finished without actively using your drone for shooting,  although using your drone gives you higher kill points/score  and also drops special skill point containers, bonuses, etc
There are a few levels which need drone support due to story/strategic reasons, but 80% should be playable without active drone shooting. 
Also, you can change your upgrades anytime by selecting the red circular wheel on the top RHS of the upgrades map. Hope this helps and we'll discuss your suggestions internally further.  Best!

I just finishe the game. I love it!
I need a part 2 or an expansion, please!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback and glad you're enjoying the game. Yes, we are looking forward to sharing  more news soon.

reviews.review_description:
I can’t get the Controls button to work in settings. Can’t change it to Left Hand Dominate. I also can’t bring the menu up using any of the buttons to build barriers. 

I think this issue is with the Quest 3. When I tried it on a Q2, it worked fine. .

developer_response.body:
Hi, the "controls" button works fine on Quest3 and indicates the functionality of each button. When you press the "controls" button, look at your controllers, each button will be labeled as to what they do. Unfortunately, you can't swap controllers' hands currently. 
Not sure what you mean by menu for "barriers", are you referring to the "roadblockers" which help you change path of the creeps? If so that's a simple click to activate action there is no menu, it can help you change the direction of enemies but only 3 times which is indicated by the 3 lights above the "roadblocker" 
Please join us on discord if you need support (or email us), and our community of players and our devs will be more than happy to help. Thanks.

reviews.review_title: Give us better hit and destroy effects!
reviews.review_description:
This is a good and very robust tower defense game, and I'd easily give it 5 stars! However, the lack of high-quality VFX effects holds it back.
Here's what I mean:
 - Hit Particles: When firing normally, there should be impactful hit particles instead of just a single hit decal.
- Charged Shot Effects: Charging a weapon for an AOE attack should result in a powerful shot with impressive particle effects, not just a scaled-up version of the normal hit decal. Imagine a volumetric explosion that conveys the area-of-effect impact!
- Death Animations: The current explosion effect for enemy units is a simple, fast, and repetitive explosion. It would be far more satisfying to see enemy units explode with debris scattering on the ground with some particle effects.
I believe that implementing higher-quality particles and explosions would significantly enhance the gameplay experience and push this game to a solid 5-star rating.

I've played a lot of tower defense games since the genre appeared in the 90s, and this one stands out only because it is in VR. I don't recommend it because it is overly simple and not challenging. Despite the wide open spaces, you have only a few predefined locations where a defensive turret can be placed. You have the option of buying blockers to marshall the attackers into a gauntlet, but that's about all the strategy you need. There are lots of buildings around, but they don't really matter because turrets can fire right through them. There is little variety in the kinds of weapons available, and even less in the way of upgrades. I would almost describe Iron Guard as casual gaming. The kind you'd pull up on your phone while waiting for your turn at the dentist. You can't even speed up the game, so you spend a lot of time standing there and waiting for the attack to play out. I didn't find it to be a particularly good use of time when so many other great games are available on the Quest.

reviews.review_title: Very grindy in a bad way....
reviews.review_description:
The good:
Looks and feels very good. The controller as a fighting and building drone is cool. The enemies are nicely modelled. The game is difficult and has 30 levels which I think is amazing.

Blockades mechanics allow you to channel the enemy the way you want.

You can reset the skills at any time.


The bad:
The game is very grindy. You only get significant skill points if you beat the level or if you beat your previous score on that level. So you can't replay a level a few times to upgrade if you're stumped at a difficult one. You also get no skill points if you lose so if you're stuck it can be very frustrating.

So for people like me that like to slowly build your arsenal and plan your strategies - that's not the game you're looking for. 
Sometimes enemies drop a skill point or two, but with later upgrades costing 300-500 it's negligible.

The levels are either easy windy ones or difficult ones where the enemy spawns right next to the base on several sides with straight access to the base. Some levels have blockades but these are in the minority. It's usually either relaxing on a windy 1-2 roads or running like crazy between 4-8 super short roads.

The vast amount of damage is made by the drone (works like a pistol you hold in your hand) but very quickly it feels like a pea shooter. There is a heavy mode where you hold the trigger but you fire by releasing it so it's very unrealiable. You can upgrade the weapons but the upgrades are something silly like 10%. Also the weapon annoyingly overheats and it doesn't tell you when it is about to shutdown so many times you looses because you fire one too many times, the weapon shuts down and the bulky enemy that you have been wearing down for the last 20 seconds gets through. Super annoying.

There MEH


The enemies are just typical fast/slow bulky/small. There's no tactics. No weapons weaknesses or similar things. It's just DPS. Just survive until you get tier 3 weapons.

The maps feel a bit repetitive.

The story is almost background noise. Not impacting the game at all.

There are two superweapons with a deliberately annoying targeting. Nothing special.


Overall the game is very intense and if you're looking for a slow paced relaxing experience I would avoid it. On the other side if replaying a difficult mission many times, squeezing the most from your meager skill points and pushing yourself through ever increasing challenges is your bread and butter, then yup should be just fine.

I think it's a nice try for TD in VR. I don't think it will convince me to play this genre this way though.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the detailed feedback.  The gameplay is designed so that you can continue till the end by clearing each level, you don't have to replay the level to progress, but you can if you want to achieve a higher score on the leaderboard or get 3 stars which gives higher skill points(SP). 
The SP is not linear, so as you finish more levels the SP also increases. There are also upgrades that automatically unlock without purchasing as you progress. As mentioned earlier you can get one star on each level and still finish the game, so replaying is optional.  
Please join us on our discord and there are many players and devs who can help with strategies and tips on difficult or unique levels with different play styles.  Best!

reviews.review_description:
You get 5 turrets.... you get some limited upgrades.... and you get a VERY limited area where you can place your turrets. 

Rather go buy Bloons TD6 on your phone for 5 bucks, it has WAY more content than this 15 dollar game, and WAY more freedom.

Edit: My review stays the same. Using the developer's logic, that gives the other game I was talking about EVEN MORE reason to choose over this game. Bloons then has over 35 x 4 (over 140 turrets if you count upgrades as seperate turrets for some reason) turrets vs this one's 5 x 3 turrets (15, if you count upgrades as a seperate turret for some reason) ,multiple heroes, which this game doesnt have, endless playtime, which bloons has immediately, The ability to place turrets literally anywhere with enemies constantly getting stronger, which this game barely has or doesnt have, for a price that is 3 times less than this game, and the ability to unlock anything ingame without extra IRL money payment.

Im sorry, but that's the reality of things, And fluffing this game up, only applies the game fluffing to the competition, and makes them look multitudes better.

Im tired of quest vr games being so short, small, expencive and limiting, wheras even phone games often are free or cheaper with so much more to do and see. It gives vr a bad, cheap quality yet expencive image rather than grow it and fulfill it's potential.

This is VR. It's supposed to be amazing. why isnt it?  Why do people cling to simplicity, when there are examples of AMAZING games for vr on pc, or examples of amazing games on phone for non vr, that should be easy to out-do in vr? whether or not People like my review, The potential to do better, is there. And the potential is absolutely amazing.

developer_response.body:
Hi, thank you for your feedback. We have 5 turrets with 3 upgrades each with varying features i.e. 15 turrets in all  with a large upgrade tree.The  platform positions and limits  was an aesthetic and strategic decision that was made to make the gameplay more exciting along with the various upgrades and features which unlock at various levels  in the game. You also have the flexibility to reset the upgrades tree and reselect your options based on your resources and replay the game with a completely different strategy. Hope you will explore the game further and also the "endless mode" for each level  which unlocks after all 30 levels are completed.  Best!          

Edit: Noted, we will share the feedback with the dev team, please feel free to join us on discord where you can discuss further with the devs directly too.

25% off https://bio.link/lordvalfromhell

Far from it: 30 great and challenging missions, very good background music and classic TD gameplay paired with the possibility of inferring opponents via a glider guarantee a long playing time. In addition, towers or the base or the glider can be upgraded and improved. I had a lot of fun with the title for a long time!

reviews.review_description:
A good tower defense game, although I would have liked to put my turrets where I want, or have more options to place them.

There a 5 turret kinds, each with 3 levels, so you can upgrade a placed turret 2 times.

Would have been nice to have seen a 'hero' to be placed on the road, holding off the enemies. And a sort of 'barracks' placement where soldiers come out to stop enemies. 

Maybe the ability to combine turrets would have been nice, stacking 2 or 3 on top of eachother, or combining them into something hybrid when you drop one on top of the other.

Overall very good. But it just could have been a bit more elaborate.

I rate this 4 stars.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review and feedback, we'll be sure to share this further with our dev team. Best!

reviews.review_description:
I really love this game. 
Addictive! 
And now they launched endless modes for each of all 30 levels!
Thank you sooooo much! 
Good job! Amazing!

developer_response.body: Thank you!! Yes, do check out the new "Endless Mode" for each 30 levels along with it's separate leaderboard. It can get quite wild and fun once you have completed all the levels and have most of the upgrades to go an "endless" onslaught of waves 😊. Best!

reviews.review_description:
The game is okay, typical tower defense, but made in VR and then no options is kind of dumb. Would be better as a flat screen game.

No real smooth turn, just smooth snap turn. Leads to me bring unable to look in the direction I want unless I turn physically which is not comfortable when your sitting down. 

No table tilt, so you have to play looking down on the game which is incredibly uncomfortable.

No left handed mode for the drone, so I have to shoot with my incredibly in-accurate right hand. 

TL:DR   while the game it's self is fun, it's incredibly unfun and irritating to play.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback and review. Will try and address some of the concerns here but pls join us on our Discord, would be happy to discuss further.

-- there is a "Smooth" Toggle under Rotation in the "Settings"menu which can be activated by pressing the "home" button on the Left controller

-- the game is a room-scale and not a tabletop gameplay, so not sure how a tilt option would be comfortable, as the entire room/battlefield would be tilted, however please try the "height" option under ``Settings" on any *Level to change your pov w.r.t the ground if you prefer that than the default, hope this helps. 
Happy Gaming!

reviews.review_description:
Hey gamers,

My dad loves this game, he’s all about tower defense games. He literally played hundreds of TD games and he literally says “I feel like a god” when he played this game!

Message me the name of the game and I’ll send you the link if the link below doesn’t work!

Delete the space after oculus, com and before and after “appreferrals” for the link to work properly!

https://www.oculus .com / appreferrals /ScoobyDoobyyDoo/5153768677983339/?utm_source=4

developer_response.body: Your dad can be god in our game anytime 😊, Thank you for sharing your kind review. Best!

reviews.review_title: About $#@& time! Get your wallets out!
reviews.review_description:
I have been a tower defense addict since the original iPhone. I've played them all across all platforms. The two best have been the original Pixel Junk Monsters (multiple platforms) and Savage Moon (PS3/PSP).

So I'm glad to see this game present a sci-fi theme like Savage Moon. Savage Moon was a great tower defense game because it also tackled the genre by using 3D space, albeit on a 2D screen. This caused some problems though as you had to constantly work the analog stick to look around and not miss anything.

But VR eliminates that issue! Because you just... look around. So I'm glad to see 3D TD continue on in VR. 

Now about THIS game! The graphics on this game are fantastic! I would've liked to see the tiniest enemies presented a little larger, they are a little hard to see from across the field of play.

The length and scope of the game are more than appropriate for the price. You definitely get your money's worth. There are plenty of upgrades and 30 levels to play. Hopefully, we'll be offered some DLC with more as well!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the glowing detailed review and feedback, very kind of you and much appreciated . Best!

reviews.review_description:
Just started playing, gameplay graphics and story are amazing. Highly detailed very fun to play and helps get the brain thinking. 

It’s fun customizing your base defense and weapons no errors or issues of any kind running on quest 2. Definitely buy this game you will be happy. 

I would like to see multiplayer in the future if possible and more updates to support further game developments. 

Thanks you made an amazing and probably the best tower defense on vr.

developer_response.body:
We love you too :) . Thank you for the review and your support. 
We are hoping to have co-op in the near future, stay tuned for updates. Until then don't forget to collect all the easter eggs scattered across random levels to unlock a special/secret achievement :) .
Best!

reviews.review_description: I like this game but wish there was more. I got the cannon maxed out first and that is all I needed to beat the game. Only three offensive towers in a TD game?  Barely past the 2 hours or I would get my money back.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the feedback, but there are 5 turret types (cannon, electric, flamethrower, rocket, laser) with 3 upgrade types for each ie. 15 turrets with variations,  please see the upgrades map under basic and advanced turrets. 

Also, maybe we misunderstood but the gameplay ie. all 30 levels cannot be completed in 2-3 hours, please join us on our discord and we're happy to discuss more.
Best regards,

reviews.review_description: Great game for passing the time, don’t have to move anything apart from your hands. The game itself is also really good, immersive and a good variety of enemies. So strange at first being in VR on a tower defence, really like it.

developer_response.body: Thank you , happy gaming!
